originalTitle: Markets brace for return to all-out war in Iran amid rising U.S. death toll, while former NATO commander warns Suez Canal could be next
summary: Retired Adm. James Stavridis told CNN on Sunday that Trump now has three options, and "and none of them are good."
content:
Stock futures were mixed Sunday evening and oil prices continued to climb as the deaths of American service members over the weekend added more fuel to the U.S.-Iran war.

Futures tied to the Dow Jones industrial average fell 61 points, or 0.12%. S&P 500 futures were down just 0.05%, but Nasdaq futures were up 0.08%.

West Texas Intermediate futures rose 2.75% to $84.76 a barrel, and Brent crude climbed 3.2% to $90.92. Gold dropped 0.53% to $3,997 per ounce.

Two U.S. troops died in Jordan from an Iranian attack, and another was missing. A third service member was killed in Iraq while attempting to dispose of a downed Iranian drone.

The deaths at the hands of the Islamic Republic would appear to cross a red line that President Donald Trump had reportedly described when he considered the threshold for ending the earlier ceasefire before signing last month's memorandum of understanding, which has since collapsed.

So far, the White House hasn't announced whether all-out war will resume. But the U.S. military is continuing its daily bombardment of Iran, with the latest salvo meant as punishment for those killed in action.

But despite more than a week of airstrikes, the U.S. military has failed to secure an alternate corridor through the Strait of Hormuz that bypasses Iran's approved route, as the regime's drones and missiles scare away commercial vessels.

Ship-tracking data shows no crossings via the U.S.-backed route, and no "shadow fleet" movements either, while Iran's channel is still seeing activity.

That gives Tehran even more leverage as global oil stockpiles keep dwindling toward critically low levels. At the same time, Iran may be deploying more advanced weapons that can evade U.S. air defenses, making bases around the Persian Gulf more vulnerable.

As a result, Trump's military options are narrowing as the U.S. military has already failed to oust the Iranian regime or break its hold over the Strait of Hormuz.

Retired Adm. James Stavridis, who served as NATO Supreme Allied Commander, told CNN on Sunday that Trump now has three options, and "and none of them are good."

First, he could simply walk away, which would produce a terrible outcome for the U.S., the Gulf, and global trade, according to Stavridis, who said this is unlikely.

The second option would be to "go big," meaning a return to the hundreds of airstrikes a day seen at the start of the war—but this time possibly adding ground troops. But he also doubted this move, given how costly it would be and the reluctance to put boots on the ground.

The third and most likely option is the current "escalate to de-escalate" stance of bombing plus tighter economic pain, but still leaving the door open to negotiations, Stavridis added.

Meanwhile, he also warned that Iran or its proxies could eventually threaten the Suez Canal, which sees even more ship traffic than the Strait of Hormuz does, and Tehran's attacks on Jordan may even be related to such plans.

"The Iranians are beginning to make noises about attempting to close that using the Houthis in the southwest corner of the Arabian Peninsula," Stavridis said. "So that ought to be in the back of everyone's mind."

This story was originally featured on Fortune.com
chineseTitle: 在美国死亡人数不断上升之际，市场准备迎接伊朗全面战争，而前北约指挥官警告苏伊士运河可能是下一个目标
chineseSummary: 退役海军上将詹姆斯·斯塔夫里迪斯周日告诉美国有线电视新闻网，特朗普现在有三个选择，“但没有一个是好的”。
chineseContent:
周日晚间，股票期货涨跌互现，油价继续攀升，因为周末美国军人的死亡给美伊战争火上浇油。

道琼斯工业平均指数期货下跌 61 点，跌幅 0.12%。标准普尔 500 指数期货仅下跌 0.05%，但纳斯达克指数期货上涨 0.08%。

西德克萨斯中质原油期货上涨 2.75%，至每桶 84.76 美元，布伦特原油期货上涨 3.2%，至每桶 90.92 美元。黄金下跌 0.53%，至每盎司 3,997 美元。

两名美军士兵在约旦死于伊朗袭击，另一名士兵失踪。第三名军人在伊拉克试图处置一架被击落的伊朗无人机时被杀。

据报道，美国总统唐纳德·特朗普在签署上个月的谅解备忘录之前考虑结束早先停火的门槛时曾描述过，伊斯兰共和国手中的死亡人数似乎跨越了红线，但该备忘录后来已破裂。

截至目前，白宫尚未宣布是否会恢复全面战争。但美国军方仍在继续对伊朗进行日常轰炸，最新的齐射旨在惩罚那些在行动中丧生的人。

但尽管进行了一个多星期的空袭，美国军方仍未能确保绕过伊朗批准的路线穿过霍尔木兹海峡的替代走廊，因为伊朗政权的无人机和导弹吓跑了商船。

船舶跟踪数据显示，没有经过美国支持的航线，也没有“影子舰队”活动，而伊朗的航道仍然存在活动。

随着全球石油库存不断减少至极低水平，这给德黑兰带来了更大的影响力。与此同时，伊朗可能正在部署更先进的武器来躲避美国的防空系统，从而使波斯湾周围的基地更加脆弱。

因此，特朗普的军事选择正在缩小，因为美国军队已经未能推翻伊朗政权或打破其对霍尔木兹海峡的控制。

曾担任北约盟军最高司令的退役海军上将詹姆斯·斯塔夫里迪斯周日告诉美国有线电视新闻网，特朗普现在有三个选择，“但没有一个是好的”。

斯塔夫里迪斯表示，首先，他可以直接走开，这会给美国、海湾地区和全球贸易带来可怕的结果，但他表示这种情况不太可能发生。

第二种选择是“大举进攻”，这意味着恢复战争开始时每天数百次的空袭，但这次可能会增加地面部队。但他也对此举表示怀疑，因为此举成本高昂，而且不愿实地考察。

斯塔夫里迪斯补充说，第三种也是最有可能的选择是当前“升级到降级”的轰炸立场以及更严重的经济痛苦，但仍为谈判敞开大门。

与此同时，他还警告说，伊朗或其代理人最终可能会威胁苏伊士运河，该运河的船舶流量甚至比霍尔木兹海峡还要多，而德黑兰对约旦的袭击甚至可能与此类计划有关。

斯塔夫里迪斯说：“伊朗人开始发出声音，试图利用胡塞武装在阿拉伯半岛西南角关闭该通道。” “所以这应该是每个人的脑海中。”

这个故事最初发表在 Fortune.com 上
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于财经市场信号，适合用于观察利率、汇率、股债商品、金融机构和宏观政策变化。
